name: Szechuan Cashew Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 Whole chicken breasts (about 2 lbs), boned and skinned
1 can (8 1/2 oz.) sliced bamboo shoots, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, whisk together the egg white, cornstarch, thin soy sauce, and white pepper. Add the chicken pieces and stir to coat evenly. Marinate in the refrigerator for 20 minutes.
2. Cut the bell pepper into same-sized pieces as the chicken. Cut the bamboo shoots into 3/4 inch lengths.
3. In a small bowl, mix together cornstarch, cold water, and thin soy sauce. Set aside.
4. Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat until very hot. Add 2 tablespoons of peanut oil and swirl to coat the pan.
5. Add the cashews and stir-fry for 1 minute or until they turn light brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels, sprinkling with salt.
6. Return the chicken to the wok and stir-fry until it turns white. Remove and set aside.
7. Add another 2 tablespoons of oil to the wok and swirl to coat. Add the chopped green onions and ginger, stirring until the ginger is light brown.
8. Return the chicken to the wok and stir. Add the bell pepper and bamboo shoots, stirring briefly.
9. Add hoisin sauce and chili paste, stirring for 1 minute. Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
10. Stir in the cornstarch mixture until thickened. Return the cashews to the wok and stir. Add the green onions and stir once more.
11. Serve hot as part of a meal.
